examples.title: Encapsulation Process Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates how data is encapsulated at each OSI layer
examples.explanation: Each layer adds its own header to the data as it moves down. The receiving end performs de-encapsulation in reverse order to extract the original data.

examples.title: Packet Forwarding at Different Layers
examples.description: Shows how devices at different layers process data based on their layer of operation
examples.code:
// DEVICE OPERATIONS BY LAYER
BEGIN
 // Physical Layer (Hub) - No addressing, just repeat
 FUNCTION Hub_Process(bits):
 SIGNAL = amplify(bits)
 OUTPUT(SIGNAL) // Sends to all ports
 
 // Data Link Layer (Switch) - Uses MAC addresses
 FUNCTION Switch_Process(frame):
 destMAC = extractMAC(frame)
 port = MAC_Table lookup(destMAC)
 OUTPUT(frame, port) // Sends to specific port
 
 // Network Layer (Router) - Uses IP addresses
 FUNCTION Router_Process(packet):
 destIP = extractIP(packet)
 routingTable = lookup(destIP)
 nextHop = getNextHop(routingTable)
 OUTPUT(packet, nextHop) // Routes to next network
END
examples.explanation: Each network device operates at specific OSI layer and makes forwarding decisions based on addresses at that layer. Higher layers can do more intelligent routing.
